These are the Fields in this layout, starting from the top left, progressing to the left then down.
This button allows you to create a new record by opening a blank Labor Posting Input Screen.  You also have the option of using the key stroke “”Control” “N””.

This button allows you to show all of the records in the labor posting table.  This table is a group of records that represents all of the hours each cabinetmaker, or other individual within the company has worked on a specific job.
Labor Posting Input Screen Help
Labor Posting Input Screen Help
This help window is for information only.  To navigate through the database you must first close this window.  To do this click on the blue oblong in the Status Area that contains the word “continue”

The Labor Posting Input Screen is primarily used to enter time worked from cabinetmaker timesheets on Monday mornings for the previous weeks work.  Every cabinetmaker fills out a time sheet with job numbers, the type of process performed  and corresponding time worked.
These are the blue buttons down the right side of this screen

Labor Report by
Craftsman

Labor Report by

Labor Sumary
Hours By Date &
Job

Labor Sumary
Hours By Date &
Job Name
Weekly Report

This button is used after all of the cabinetmaker hours have been input for a specific weeks period of time.  By clicking on this button you activate a window that prompts you to enter a date or a range of dates.  The report that is displayed is all of the time that was entered, by cabinetmaker, based on the date you entered.  Normally you would use this to verify the time for a week ending date that corresponds to the hours you have just entered from the time sheets.  This is a way of verifying that your input is correct.  

After you are done viewing this report you will close this report by clicking on the blue “Continue” oblong in the status area.  You will then be prompted “Do you want to print this report?”  If you click on “NO” it will take you back to the Labor Posting Input Screen.  If you click on “YES” it will bring up the print screen where you can designate what printer you want to print this report on.  After you click on “Print” the system will take you back to the Labor Posting Input Screen.

Rate
In this field you will type in the number of the job you will be recording the hours for.  Once you have entered the job number you will tab to the next field.
The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system, based on the job number.  When you tab after entering the job number this field will be skipped.
The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system, based on the job number.  When you tab after entering the job number this field will be skipped.

This is the next field you will enter information into.  This field is a drop down menu.  Click on the person’s name you are entering the information for.  The system will automatically take you to the next field for input once you click on your choice.

This date, if you are entering timesheets, should be the week ending date for those timesheets.  Make sure you enter the same date for all the timesheets.  The format you should use for all dates is as follows:  MM/DD/YY.

This field is a drop down menu.  Click on the type of work the cabinetmaker has indicated on their timesheet.  The system will automatically take you to the next field for input once you click on your choice.

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  For this screen the “Type” is always “Labor” This field will be skipped, you will not be able to change the contents of this field.

After you have determined the time for this job, for this cabinetmaker doing this process, you will enter the time here.  You should use the following formats:  quarter of an hour = .25, half an hour = .50, three-quarters of an hour = .75. Time representing hours with minutes should be entered as; 1.50, 1.25, 1.75 etc.  Do not use other fractions.  From here the system supplies the information for the remaining fields.
The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  At the time this posting was originally entered this was the current labor rate.
The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  This field is computed by the system based on the hours entered times the “Original Labor Rate”.
The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system. This is the labor rate being used as of the current date.

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  This field is computed by the system based on the hours entered times “Today’s Labor Rate”.  This allows us to see what the labor costs were when the job was first done compared to what it would take to do the same work at the current established labor rate.

Once you have entered all of the information for a record, you may progress;

1 - To a new blank record by; keyboard strokes “Control N” or using the button “Create New Record”
2 - To a new layout or menu by; going to the status area and accessing the oblong under the “Layout” heading; by depressing the blue arrow and clicking on your choice of layout you wish to go to next
3 - Hide the system from view so you can work on something else; on the main navagation bar at the top of your screen you will see the words “Filemaker Pro”, if you click on this word a drop down menu of actions will appear.  Click on the entry “hide Filemaker Pro” and the screen will disappear into your dock area.
